title: ZIP-302 module comment incorrectly states first memo byte is always 0x35
description: memo.rs line 15: 'The first byte is always 0x35 ('5')'. Wrong: a UUID starting with '0' has first byte 0x30, 'a' → 0x61, etc. The debug_assert checks memo[0] <= 0xF4, which IS correct for all ASCII hex digits (max 'f' = 0x66 < 0xF4). The assertion is fine; the comment is wrong. Fix: change comment to 'The first byte is always an ASCII hex digit (0x30-0x39 or 0x61-0x66), well below the 0xF4 threshold.'
